qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default-disabled OAuth2 allows unauthenticated access to all UDR subscriber data endpoints
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns default-disabled OAuth2 authorization behavior across UDR endpoints; it does not identify the invalid influenceId path-validation branch or missing return after the 404 response in HandleApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdPut.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Concurrent map access in subscription handlers causes fatal panic and denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unsynchronized concurrent access to UDR subscription maps causing a Go runtime panic; it does not identify the advisory's missing return after invalid influenceId handling in the Traffic Influence Subscription PUT handler.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: User-controlled notification URLs in subscription data enable server-side request forgery
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unvalidated notificationUri and callback URLs later used for outbound callback requests; although it references influence data callbacks, it does not identify the advisory's invalid influenceId path segment bypass or the write-after-404 missing return.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause: HandleApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdPut sends a 404 for influenceId values other than subs-to-notify but continues execution and writes the Traffic Influence Subscription.
evidence: The advisory root cause is the missing return after c.String(http.StatusNotFound, "404 page not found") in NFs/udr/internal/sbi/api_datarepository.go, function HandleApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdPut.
CWE-284-auth-bypass-default-no-auth reports default OAuth2Required=false authorization behavior, not the invalid influenceId branch or write-after-404 control flow.
CWE-362-concurrent-map-panic-dos reports unsynchronized Go map access causing process crash, not improper path validation in the influenceData PUT handler.
CWE-918-ssrf-notification-uri reports unvalidated notification callback URLs and outbound requests, not the missing return that permits creation or overwrite through an invalid influenceId path.
